rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 13, 2017, line 4. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Moot. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS GONZALEZ VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 13, 2017, line 4. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. As to causes of action for Fraud and Breach of Fiduciary Duty Plaintiff shall have 10-days to amend the complaint and alleged facts supporting each element of delayed discovery. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority in support of its argument re: applicability of equitable estoppel under the circumstances of this case. Additionally, as to the Breach of Fiduciary Duty cause of action Plaintiff shall have 10-day leave to amend to allege in good faith this cause of action without allegations sounding in fraud (unless Plaintiff can sufficiently allege delayed discovery). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 13, 2017, line 8. CROSS DEFENDANT ALLISON LESHEFSKY's AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT Continued to April 26, 2017 per stipulation and order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LILY TIEN VS. YU YUN TIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 13, 2017, line 9. CROSS DEFENDANT LILY TIEN's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. The cross-complaint is brought by a non-party against Plaintiff and a non-party. Cross-complainant does not cite any authority for the proposition that "an individual" and "a trustee" are the same party by virtue of being "real parties in interest" each in their own right as to separate claims. Cross-complaint does not arise out of the same transaction/occurrence as the Complaint. Cross-complainant does not substantively address cross-defendant's argument that cross-claims belong in Probate Department. Meet and confer requirements have been sufficiently complied with. = (501/REQ)
